The '''atelic perfect''' is one of the two perfect aspects in Aelaki (alongside the [[Telic|telic perfect]]). It expresses the present result of an unbounded action -- one without an inherent endpoint.

== Formation ==

The atelic perfect is formed through an '''o-grade''' vowel shift in the stem.

Using the root ''zh-r-n'' "live" (base form: ''zharan''):

{| class="wikitable"
! Aspect !! Form !! Pattern !! Meaning
|-
| Base || zharan || C₁V₁C₂V₂C₃ || (someone) lives
|-
| Atelic perfect || zhoron || o-grade || is alive (state achieved)
|}

The atelic perfect focuses on the resulting state of an action that has no defined completion point. ''Zhoron'' emphasizes the current state of being alive, without implying that the action of living has concluded.

== Present tense: Habitual ==

In the present tense, the atelic perfect form takes on a '''[[Habitual|habitual]]''' meaning, expressing repeated or customary actions.

{| class="wikitable"
! Tense !! Form !! Meaning
|-
| Past || zhoron || is alive (state achieved)
|-
| Present || zhoron || lives (habitually)
|}

== Contrast with telic perfect ==

{| class="wikitable"
! Aspect !! Form !! Meaning !! Telicity
|-
| Atelic perfect || zhoron || is alive (state achieved) || unbounded, no inherent endpoint
|-
| [[Telic|Telic perfect]] || zheren || lived (life completed) || bounded, goal reached
|}

== See also ==

* [[Telic]]
* [[Perfect]]
* [[Habitual]]

[[Category:Continuities?]]
